info.title: LCC Deal Intelligence
info.description: Canonical Swagger 2.0 spec for the LCC Deal Intelligence custom connector in Power Platform. 87 operations. x-ms-requestBody-name added to all POST body params to fix PowerFxJsonException.

apiKey.name: X-LCC-Key
apiKey.description: LCC API key for Power Automate / Copilot

Count and list LIVE government deals by state from the live database — currently
post.description: **Tier 0** (read-only). Category: portfolio. Count and list LIVE government deals by state from the live database — currently-available listings and sales closed within the past N months. Use this for any quantitative or geographic deal question (e.g. "how many gov deals are available in Texas", "what sold in Florida in the last 12 months"). Returns exact live counts; prefer this over knowledge-file lookups for current numbers.

state.description: US state, 2-letter code or full name (e.g. "TX" or "Texas").

months.description: Look-back window in months for sold deals (default: 12).

limit.description: Max sample rows to return per category (default: 10, max: 25).

available_count.description: Exact count of active listings in the state.

sold_count.description: Exact count of sales in the look-back window.

post.summary: Count and list LIVE government deals by state from the live database — currently

months.description: Look-back window in months for sold deals (default: 12).

limit.description: Max sample rows to return per category (default: 10, max: 25).

available_count.description: Exact count of active listings in the state.

sold_count.description: Exact count of sales in the look-back window.

Search the LCC entity graph and GOV contacts database by name or keyword. Return
post.description: **Tier 0** (read-only). Category: outreach. Search the LCC entity graph and GOV contacts database by name or keyword. Returns matching entities/contacts with IDs, emails, and details. Use this to resolve a person or company name before calling draft_outreach_email (use the returned contact_id) or get_entity_context (use the returned entity_id).

query.description: Name, company, or keyword to search for (min 2 characters)

entity_type.description: Optional: filter LCC entities by type

entities.description: Matching entities/contacts. Each item has: entity_id (for get_entity_context), contact_id (for draft_outreach_email), name, entity_type, email, company, domain, source.

entity_id.description: LCC entity UUID — use with get_entity_context

contact_id.description: GOV contacts unified_id — use with draft_outreach_email

post.summary: Draft a personalized outreach email for a business development contact and autom
post.description: **Tier 1** (lightweight-confirm) — requires explicit confirmation. Category: outreach. Draft a personalized outreach email for a business development contact and automatically save it to Outlook Drafts via Power Automate. Always creates an Outlook draft when a recipient email is available. ALWAYS pass contact_name when known — it drives LCC relationship enrichment and email personalization. Pass to with the recipient email. You do NOT need contact_id if you have contact_name. Pass text_only=true ONLY if the user explicitly asks to preview text without saving to Outlook.

contact_id.description: Contact unified_id from GOV contacts DB (optional — use if available from a prior search)

contact_name.description: Contact full name — ALWAYS pass this when the person's name is known, even without a contact_id (required)

intent.description: Purpose of the outreach (e.g., "reconnect", "listing pitch", "market update")

tone.description: Desired tone (default: professional, warm, and concise)

to.description: Recipient email address (required). Draft saves to Outlook Drafts automatically when present.

text_only.description: Set true ONLY when user explicitly asks to preview text without saving to Outlook.

draft_web_link.description: Link that opens the created Outlook draft.

post.summary: Draft a personalized outreach email for a business development contact and autom

contact_id.description: Contact unified_id from GOV contacts DB (optional — use if available from a prior search)

contact_name.description: Contact full name — ALWAYS pass this when the person's name is known, even without a contact_id (required)

intent.description: Purpose of the outreach (e.g., "reconnect", "listing pitch", "market update")

tone.description: Desired tone (default: professional, warm, and concise)

to.description: Recipient email address (required). Draft saves to Outlook Drafts automatically when present.

text_only.description: Set true ONLY when user explicitly asks to preview text without saving to Outlook.

draft_web_link.description: Link that opens the created Outlook draft.

to.description: Seller recipient email address (required). Draft saves to Outlook Drafts automatically when present.

draft_web_link.description: Link that opens the created Outlook draft.

post.summary: Draft a context-aware reply to a flagged email already in the LCC inbox. Provide
post.description: **Tier 1** (lightweight-confirm) — requires explicit confirmation. Category: outreach. Draft a context-aware reply to a flagged email already in the LCC inbox. Provide inbox_item_id (UUID from inbox_items). The recipient is derived automatically from the original sender. Set create_draft=true to also create the reply as a real draft in the user's Outlook (returns draft_web_link).

inbox_item_id.description: UUID of the inbox_items row (the flagged email).

intent.description: Purpose of the reply (e.g., "answer the question", "schedule a call", "request more info", "decline politely"). Drives the call-to-action.

tone.description: Desired tone (default: professional, warm, and responsive).

guidance.description: Optional freeform instructions — facts to include, points to address, things to avoid.

create_draft.description: If true, create the reply as a real draft in Outlook (recipient derived from the original sender).

original_sender.description: Email address the reply will be sent to.

draft_web_link.description: Link that opens the created Outlook draft.

post.summary: Draft a context-aware reply to a flagged email already in the LCC inbox. Provide

inbox_item_id.description: UUID of the inbox_items row (the flagged email).

intent.description: Purpose of the reply (e.g., "answer the question", "schedule a call", "request more info", "decline politely"). Drives the call-to-action.

tone.description: Desired tone (default: professional, warm, and responsive).

guidance.description: Optional freeform instructions — facts to include, points to address, things to avoid.

create_draft.description: If true, create the reply as a real draft in Outlook (recipient derived from the original sender).

original_sender.description: Email address the reply will be sent to.

draft_web_link.description: Link that opens the created Outlook draft.

post.summary: Ingest an uploaded PDF into the LCC intake queue for triage. Used when a user up
post.description: **Tier 1** (lightweight-confirm) — requires lightweight confirmation. Category: workflow. Ingest an uploaded PDF into the LCC intake queue for triage. Used when a user uploads a document in Copilot chat (deed, offering memorandum, lease, brochure, financials, etc.) and wants it captured into LCC.

file_content_base64.description: Base64-encoded PDF content. Provide this OR file_url.

file_url.description: Publicly accessible HTTPS download URL (SharePoint or OneDrive share link, direct PDF URL). Provide this OR file_content_base64.

entity_id.description: Optional UUID of a property or organization this document relates to. Pre-links the intake item to that entity.

note.description: Optional one-line user description of what the document is.

message.description: Human-readable summary for Copilot to display to the user.

Promote an inbox item to a shared team action item.
post.description: **Tier 2** (explicit-confirm) — requires explicit confirmation. Category: workflow. Promote an inbox item to a shared team action item.

inbox_item_id.description: UUID of the inbox_items row to promote to an active action (required).

title.description: Action item title — defaults to the intake item title if omitted.

action_type.description: Type of action to create: follow_up, research, review, outreach, or meeting (default: follow_up).

priority.description: Task priority level: low, normal, high, or urgent (default: normal).

assigned_to.description: UUID of the team member to assign the action to (optional — defaults to the current user).

due_date.description: Target due date in YYYY-MM-DD format (optional).

entity_id.description: Entity UUID to link this action to (optional — defaults to the intake item entity).

action_type.description: Type of follow-up: follow_up, call, email, research, meeting, or review (default: follow_up).

priority.description: Task priority: low, normal, high, or urgent (default: normal).

due_date.description: Target completion date in YYYY-MM-DD format (optional).

description.description: Additional notes or context for the task (optional).

post.summary: Query de-duplicated sales comps across gov DB, dialysis DB, and Salesforce.
post.description: Returns de-duplicated, cap-normalized comps blended from the government DB, dialysis DB, and Salesforce. Present the markdown field verbatim; never substitute SharePoint or proxy comps.

property_types.description: plain terms: medical, office, retail, industrial, dialysis, government

date_from.description: ISO date; from any time window

markdown.description: Pre-rendered comp table — display this field VERBATIM as the answer.

post.summary: Assemble one ranked, de-duplicated comp set from a plain-language request.
post.description: Returns de-duplicated, cap-normalized comps blended from the government DB, dialysis DB, and Salesforce. Present the markdown field verbatim; never substitute SharePoint or proxy comps.

request.description: Original plain-language request (used for government-intent routing).

property_types.description: plain terms: medical, office, retail, industrial, dialysis, government

date_from.description: ISO date; from any time window

markdown.description: Pre-rendered comp table — display this field VERBATIM as the answer.
